methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Genespire is a Series B biotech company founded by Luigi Naldini and Alessio Cantore that has raised $52M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private biotech firms at the same stage and 3 public-market biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $99M. Peer-median valuation anchors to five same-sector, same-stage companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—producing a median of $810M. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (6.6x across biotech peers) is not applicable absent disclosed revenue figures. 

The triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $84M–$931M, with a median of $454M. The wide dispersion reflects stage-progression's reliance on historical funding rounds versus peer-median's dependence on market sentiment for biotech innovators. The latter method dominates given Genespire's positioning within an active peer cohort. Key uncertainties include asset-stage maturity relative to comparables and whether the peer median adequately reflects company-specific de-risking post-Series A.
